name: Counterparty 2
description: The allowable value for Counterparty 2 is an LEI or another identifier determined in accordance with Rule S1.3.1(2). In the case of a transaction traded on a facility and cleared by a CCP, and the identities of the counterparties are not disclosed to each other, 'ANON' is reported. Another identifier may be a Designated Business Identifier (such as an ABN, AVID or BIC) or, if unavailable, a unique Client Code in the format and structure of the LEI of the Reporting Entity (or of the Execution agent) followed by up to 52 characters. An LEI of Counterparty 2 must not be an LEI of a branch of the entity.
format: ISO 17442 Legal entity identifier (LEI); Alphanumeric code up to 52 characters; ABN; AVID; BIC; 'ANON'
valueRules: If LEI is reported, it must be a valid GLEIF LEI of the required registration status and pertain to a legal entity and not a branch.
If a non-LEI identifier is reported, the format of the value must conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
For Action types NEWT, MODI, CORR, the LEI registration status must be 'Issued', 'Lapsed', 'Pending transfer' or 'Pending archival'.
For Action types REVI, TERM, EROR, PRTO, the LEI registration status can be any status.
May be 'ANON' for transactions traded on a facility and cleared by a CCP where counterparties are not disclosed.

citations.document_name: ASIC Derivative Transaction Rules (Reporting) 2024—Schedule 1 technical guidance
